image.name: Image Dataset
image.description: CIFAR-100 image classification with CNN, ViT, and Ensemble models

results.summary: Image classification results on CIFAR-100 test dataset: Ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16 models

confusionMatrix.description: Confusion matrix for CIFAR-100 classification showing model performance across 100 classes
confusionMatrix.note: 100 classes across 20 supercategories (e.g., aquatic mammals, fish, flowers, vehicles, etc.)

observations: ViT-B/16 outperformed ResNet50 (81.27% vs 55.01% accuracy) due to better global context modeling
Ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16 achieved 81.19% accuracy, combining local texture and global attention strengths
ViT showed greater robustness to Gaussian noise (10% std) with only 2% accuracy drop compared to CNN's 5% drop
MobileNetV3-small struggled with CIFAR-100 (1.17% accuracy), highlighting challenges for lightweight architectures on fine-grained classification

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem and Dataset Analysis
items: CIFAR-100 dataset analysis: 60,000 32x32 color images in 100 classes across 20 supercategories
Perfect class balance with exactly 500 training images per class
Channel imbalance analysis: Blue channel darker, prevalence of white backgrounds
Spatial center bias identified through average image analysis

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Dataset, DataLoader, and Augmentation Techniques
items: Image preprocessing: resize to 224x224, normalization with CIFAR-100 mean/std
Data augmentation: random horizontal flip (p=0.5), color jitter (brightness, contrast, saturation, hue)
Train/Test split: 50,000 training images, 10,000 test images
DataLoader configuration with batch size 128 and pin_memory for GPU acceleration

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: Construction, Training, Evaluation, and Comparison of CNN, ViT, and Ensemble Models
items: Model architectures: ResNet50 (CNN), ViT-B/16 (Vision Transformer), MobileNetV3-small (lightweight CNN)
Transfer learning: Pretrained ImageNet backbones frozen, only final classification head trained
Training: Adam optimizer (lr=0.001), cross-entropy loss, 5 epochs
Evaluation metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score (macro average)
Ensemble method: Soft voting averaging probabilities from ResNet50 and ViT-B/16

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Tables, Charts, Analysis, and Discussion
items: Quantitative results comparison: ResNet50 (55.01%), ViT-B/16 (81.27%), MobileNetV3 (1.17%), Ensemble (81.19%)
Confusion matrix visualization for ViT-B/16 showing error patterns across 100 classes
Error analysis: Top 5 most confused class pairs (e.g., 'apple' vs 'pear', 'dolphin' vs 'whale')
Training curves: Loss and accuracy over epochs for all models
Robustness evaluation: Gaussian noise (10% std) impact on model performance

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Additional Experiments/Improvements
items: Interpretability: Grad-CAM visualizations for ResNet50, attention maps for ViT-B/16
Robustness evaluation: Gaussian noise injection to test model robustness
Ensemble methods: Soft voting ensemble of ResNet50 and ViT-B/16
Efficiency analysis: Parameter count vs inference time vs accuracy trade-offs
Comparative analysis: ViT's superior performance despite higher computational cost

results.summary: Text classification results on IMDB sentiment dataset:
observations: LSTM model achieved 89.2% accuracy on IMDB test set
Model better at identifying positive reviews (91% precision)
Negative reviews had lower recall (87%) due to sarcasm/irony

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem and Dataset Analysis
items: IMDB dataset analysis: 50,000 movie reviews with binary sentiment labels
Class distribution analysis: balanced (25,000 positive, 25,000 negative)
Text length analysis and vocabulary size statistics
Exploratory data visualization: word frequency, sentiment distribution

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Dataset, DataLoader, and Augmentation Techniques
items: Text preprocessing: tokenization, stopword removal, lowercasing
Sequence padding/truncation to fixed length (500 tokens)
Word embeddings: GloVe embeddings (300-dimensional)
Train/Validation/Test split: 40,000/5,000/5,000 reviews
DataLoader configuration with batch size 32 and shuffling

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: Construction, Training, Evaluation, and Comparison of Models
items: RNN/LSTM architecture: embedding layer → LSTM → dense layers
Model 1: Simple LSTM with 128 hidden units
Model 2: Bidirectional LSTM with attention mechanism
Training: Adam optimizer (lr=0.001), cross-entropy loss, 20 epochs
Evaluation metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Tables, Charts, Analysis, and Discussion
items: Quantitative results comparison between LSTM and BiLSTM models
Confusion matrix visualization for sentiment classification
Error analysis: misclassification patterns in sarcastic/ironic reviews
Training curves: loss and accuracy over epochs
Discussion: impact of word embeddings and attention mechanisms

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Additional Experiments/Improvements
items: Alternative models: BERT, Transformer-based classifiers
Hyperparameter optimization: hidden size, dropout, learning rate
Ensemble methods: combining LSTM and Transformer predictions
Transfer learning with pre-trained language models
Computational efficiency: inference time and memory usage

results.summary: Multimodal classification results on test dataset:
observations: Late fusion outperformed early fusion by 3.2%
Text features complemented visual features for ambiguous cases

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem and Dataset Analysis
items: Multimodal dataset analysis: combined image-text pairs with labels
Dataset statistics: number of samples, modality alignment verification
Exploratory analysis: visual features vs textual features correlation
Challenge analysis: modality gap, missing modalities, noise

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Dataset, DataLoader, and Augmentation Techniques
items: Image preprocessing: resize, normalization, augmentation (flip, rotation)
Text preprocessing: tokenization, embedding extraction
Modality alignment: ensuring image-text pair consistency
Train/Validation/Test split: maintaining modality balance
DataLoader configuration for heterogeneous multimodal batches

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: Construction, Training, Evaluation, and Comparison of Fusion Methods
items: Multimodal fusion architectures: early fusion, late fusion, hybrid fusion
Model 1: Early fusion with concatenated features
Model 2: Late fusion with separate encoders and attention mechanism
Training: Adam optimizer (lr=0.001), cross-entropy loss, 30 epochs
Evaluation metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Tables, Charts, Analysis, and Discussion
items: Quantitative results comparison between different fusion strategies
Confusion matrix visualization for multimodal classification
Ablation study: impact of individual modalities on performance
Training curves: loss and accuracy over epochs
Discussion: effectiveness of attention mechanisms for cross-modal alignment

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Additional Experiments/Improvements
items: Alternative fusion methods: transformer-based cross-attention
Hyperparameter optimization: fusion layer size, dropout rates
Modality dropout experiments for robustness testing
Transfer learning with pre-trained visual and language models
Computational efficiency: trade-offs between fusion complexity and accuracy

technicalSummary.title: Technical Summary
techniques: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for image feature extraction
Vision Transformers (ViT) for global context modeling
Ensemble methods (soft voting) for combining CNN and ViT predictions
Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN/LSTM) for sequential text data
Multimodal fusion architectures for combining image and text features
Data Augmentation (rotation, flipping, zoom for images; synonym replacement for text)
Transfer Learning using pre-trained models (ResNet, VGG, BERT, GPT)
technicalSummary.architecture:
Image Branch: CNN (ResNet50) and Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16)
Text Branch: LSTM with word embeddings
Multimodal Fusion: Concatenation + Dense layers
Output: Softmax classification

Training: Adam optimizer, learning rate 0.001, batch size 128
